nativeRange.habitat: Generally found on clear lagoon and seaward reefs, typically occurring in loose aggregations near caves and ledges. Has been documented as abundant over branching corals and on leeward coasts, at depths ranging from approximately 11 to 120 m.

diet.notes: Planktivorous in the wild, with a recorded trophic level of approximately 3.0, feeding on zooplankton in midwater aggregations above reef structures. In captivity it has been reported to accept a varied diet of meaty frozen foods (mysis shrimp, brine shrimp), herbivore flakes, and frozen preparations. Vitamin-enriched foods are generally advised to help maintain colouration.

summary: A small, peaceful schooling damselfish from the Indian Ocean reef, recognised by its golden-yellow body and violet-pink facial markings. Generally considered reef-safe and well-suited to groups of three or more in an established reef aquarium.
careNotes:
The Agile Chromis is found throughout the Indian Ocean, ranging from the East African coast (Kenya to Mozambique), Madagascar, and the Comoro Islands to the Maldives, Seychelles, and Chagos Archipelago.

In the wild this species tends to form loose aggregations in the mid-water column near caves, ledges, and branching corals on clear lagoon and seaward reefs, typically at depths of 11–120 m.

The Agile Chromis is generally considered a peaceful fish, though mild territorial behaviour toward conspecifics (members of the same species) has been reported, particularly in small groups or in aquariums with insufficient hiding places. Keeping three or more individuals together with adequate rockwork tends to distribute dominance interactions across the group and is broadly recommended.

As a schooling species that needs both open mid-water swimming space and multiple retreat areas, a tank of at least 120 L is generally advised to house a small group comfortably.

The Agile Chromis has been reported to accept a broad range of prepared and frozen foods readily in captivity, and is generally considered an easy feeder.
breedingNotes: Oviparous with distinct pair bonding during breeding. Demersal adhesive eggs are deposited on hard substrate and guarded and aerated by the male. Captive breeding is rarely documented in the hobby literature.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary husbandry reference: max size 3 in (7.6 cm), minimum tank 30 gal (114 L), temperature 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C), pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, care level easy, reef compatible yes, diet omnivore (meaty items, herbivore flakes, frozen preparations), temperament peaceful, group of 3 or more recommended. additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Pomacentridae, Ovalentaria/misc; reclassified as Pycnochromis agilis), native range (Indian Ocean: Maldives, Chagos, Seychelles, Madagascar, Comoros, East African coast), maximum length 6.1 cm SL, depth range 11–120 m, habitat (clear lagoon and seaward reefs, near caves and branching corals), trophic level 3.0 (omnivore/planktivore), oviparous breeding with male egg-guarding, IUCN Least Concern. No water parameter data (temperature, pH, salinity) provided by FishBase for this species — husbandry parameters sourced from LiveAquaria only.

fish.breedingNotes: Oviparous, with distinct pair bonding during breeding. Demersal adhesive eggs are deposited on hard substrate and guarded and aerated by the male until hatching. Captive spawning is rarely documented in the hobby, and rearing the pelagic larvae is considered difficult.
